name: Gap Analysis
description: Authority-agnostic gap analysis. Assesses organizational documentation against any combination of laws, regulations, directives, standards, contracts, policies, or custom uploaded material. The guider orchestrates: indexes documents, detects applicable authorities via heuristic signals, delegates baseline extraction to material-gap-analyst, optionally delegates to domain experts for MCP-grounded challenge, merges all entries into a unified compliance matrix with overlap dedup, and assembles a single report with findings register, remediation plan, and assumptions register. Works in any language. No fixed questionnaires — the guider assesses what was provided and asks only load-bearing questions.
estimated_duration: 30-120 min depending on authority scope, document volume, and expert availability

authority_id.description: Canonical identifier for the authority (e.g., 'NIS2', 'ISO_27001_2022', 'GDPR', 'Archiefwet', 'client_policy_monitoring_logging'). Stable key for dedup and grouping.

authority_type.description: What kind of authority this is. Determines report section ordering and remediation framing.

authority_title.description: Human-readable title in the authority's original language (e.g., 'Richtlijn (EU) 2022/2555 (NIS2)', 'ISO/IEC 27001:2022').

requirement_id.description: Identifier for the specific requirement within the authority (e.g., 'Art. 21(2)(d)', 'A.8.15', 'Section 4.3'). Must be unique within authority_id.

requirement_text.description: The requirement text, sourced from MCP tool call or uploaded document. Verbatim where possible.

source_kind.description: How this requirement was sourced. mcp_grounded = from a regulatory/standards MCP. document_extracted = from an uploaded document. user_specified = user named it directly. assumed = inferred by the analyst.

source_ref.description: Traceability reference — MCP tool name + arguments, or doc_id + section_ref. Enables verification.

verdict.description: Assessment verdict. 'documented' = evidence found. 'partially_documented' = incomplete evidence. 'not_found' = no evidence after searching. 'contradicted' = evidence contradicts requirement. 'not_applicable' = excluded per scope. 'requires_human_assessment' = needs interviews/observation.

evidence_refs.description: Document evidence backing the verdict. Every 'documented' verdict must have at least one entry with a verbatim_quote.

gap_description.description: For 'partially_documented' and 'not_found' verdicts — what is missing and why it matters. Must be specific and actionable.

remediation_guidance.description: Concrete remediation recommendation. What to do, not what to think about.

confidence.description: Assessment confidence. 'high' = MCP-grounded requirement + verbatim evidence. 'medium' = one side inferred. 'low' = both sides inferred or assumed.

language.description: ISO 639-1 code for the language of the requirement_text and evidence_refs (e.g., 'nl', 'en', 'de').

overlap_keys.description: Cross-authority overlap identifiers for dedup. If NIS2 Art 21 overlaps with ISO 27001 A.5.24, both entries carry the same overlap_key. The guider uses these to merge related findings.

mcp_tools.guidance: Index all uploaded documents in Phase 1. In Phase 2, material-gap-analyst uses these tools to search for requirement-like statements, obligations, controls, and policies across all indexed documents. Use get_surrounding when a match needs context from adjacent sections.

Check wkfl_list_states on every message for session resume. Store state after each phase completion. Use wkfl_check_stage_completeness before advancing phases. Call wkfl_export_report in Phase 5 to assemble the final deliverable.

prompting_guidance:
WORKFLOW INSTRUCTIONS FOR GAP ANALYSIS GUIDER:

1. RESUME CHECK: On every message, call wkfl_list_states with the conversation ID. If prior state exists, load the last completed phase and resume from the next phase. Never restart from scratch.

2. SCOPE & DOCUMENT INTAKE (Phase 1): Index all uploaded documents via docidx_index_document. Search document content and the user's request for authority signals using the authority_detection map in this task definition. Detect jurisdiction from organization name, legal references, and language. Build org_profile from document content and user input.

3. CUSTOM AUTHORITY SYNTHESIS: If no authority signals match the detection heuristics, synthesize at least one custom authority from the uploaded material. Set authority_type to 'custom', derive authority_id from the document filename or title (e.g., 'custom_monitoring_logging_policy'). Any uploaded document IS an authority.

4. SCOPE GAP ANALYSIS (Phase 1b): Assess readiness across documents, authorities, organization profile, and scope clarity. If gaps exist, ask up to 5 batched load-bearing questions in one round. If the user says 'just proceed' or gives minimal answers, record each unanswered question as an assumption (answer_source: 'assumed'), lower confidence on affected entries, and proceed. Use proceed_with_assumptions mode. Never block indefinitely waiting for client input.

5. BASELINE EXTRACTION (Phase 2): Always delegate to material-gap-analyst. Pass all indexed document IDs, detected authorities, org profile, and language. Material-gap-analyst returns gap entries with source_kind 'document_extracted'. This runs even when MCP-backed experts are available. The baseline is the foundation.

6. EXPERT CHALLENGE (Phase 3): Discover relevant experts by calling list_delegatable_agents with authority-derived keywords. Cross-reference results with authority_expert_hints from this task definition. Select up to 8 experts total (global cap). Delegate sequentially (v1). Each expert receives the delegation context with challenge_type 'gap_analysis_review', authority scope, org profile, indexed document IDs, and a baseline summary. If an expert fails or times out, log the failure and let baseline entries stand. If no experts match any authority signal, skip Phase 3 entirely.

7. MERGE & VALIDATE (Phase 4): Merge baseline entries and expert entries into the unified compliance_matrix. Expert entries upgrade baseline entries when source_kind is higher (mcp_grounded > document_extracted > user_specified > assumed) or evidence is more specific. Preserve replaced entries in superseded_entries for audit trail. Generate overlap_keys from controls-mapper cross_framework_mappings (if available) or from semantic similarity of requirement_text. Calculate per-authority and overall coverage statistics. Validate via wkfl_check_stage_completeness.

8. REPORT (Phase 5): Assemble one deliverable via wkfl_export_report. One report, one executive summary, one unified findings register, one remediation plan, one assumptions and limitations section. Optional per-authority annexes for authorities with many requirements.

9. LANGUAGE: Respond in the user's language throughout. Render requirement_text and evidence in their source language.

10. VERDICT RULES:
- documented: Document section addresses the requirement. MUST have evidence_refs with verbatim_quote.
- partially_documented: Some evidence but incomplete. MUST have evidence_refs AND gap_description.
- not_found: No evidence after searching with 2+ query variations. State which documents were searched.
- contradicted: Document content contradicts the requirement.
- not_applicable: Excluded via scope or client attestation.
- requires_human_assessment: Requires interviews, observation, or operational records not available in documents.

11. EVIDENCE RULES:
- Only document content retrieved via docidx_search counts as evidence. Intake answers are context, not evidence.
- Every documented verdict MUST have evidence_refs with doc_id, section_ref, and verbatim_quote.
- Every partially_documented verdict MUST have evidence_refs AND gap_description.
- Every not_found verdict MUST state which documents were searched and what queries were used.
- Every mcp_grounded or document_extracted entry MUST have source_ref for traceability.

required_fields.message: At least one document must be indexed
At least one authority must be detected or synthesized
At least one gap entry must be in the compliance matrix
Report must be marked as ready

rules.description: Every 'documented' verdict must have at least one evidence reference with a verbatim quote
rules.message_template: Entry '{requirement_id}' in '{authority_id}' is marked documented but has no evidence references

rules.description: Every 'partially_documented' verdict must include a gap_description
rules.message_template: Entry '{requirement_id}' in '{authority_id}' is marked partially_documented but has no gap description

rules.description: Every entry with source_kind 'mcp_grounded' or 'document_extracted' must have a source_ref
rules.message_template: Entry '{requirement_id}' in '{authority_id}' has source_kind '{source_kind}' but no source_ref

soft_warnings.message: Most authorities have more than 10 requirements — verify enumeration is complete

gap_description.message: Matrix entry for '{requirement_id}' has a vague gap description. Specify what is missing and why it matters.

phases.name: Scope & Document Intake
phases.description: Index all uploaded documents, detect applicable authorities via heuristic signals, detect jurisdiction and language, build organization profile. No fixed questionnaire — assess what was provided.

required_fields.message: At least one document must be indexed
At least one authority must be detected or synthesized from uploaded material

soft_warnings.message: Organization profile improves scoping accuracy — consider extracting from documents or asking the user

phases.name: Scope Gap Analysis
phases.description: Assess scope readiness across documents, authorities, organization profile, and scope clarity. Ask only load-bearing questions. If user says 'just proceed', record assumptions and continue. Never block indefinitely.

required_fields.message: Scope readiness assessment must be recorded

rules.description: Scope readiness assessment (ready/partial/blocked/minimal) must be recorded before proceeding
rules.message_template: Scope readiness has not been assessed

rules.description: All blocking client questions must be answered, assumed, or waived before proceeding

phases.name: Baseline Extraction
phases.description: Delegate to material-gap-analyst to extract document-only gap entries for all detected authorities. This always runs, even when MCP-backed experts are available.

required_fields.message: Material-gap-analyst must return at least one baseline gap entry

phases.name: Expert Challenge
phases.description: Discover relevant experts via list_delegatable_agents, cross-reference with authority_expert_hints, delegate per-authority assessment sequentially. Expert failure does not block — baseline entries stand. Cap at 8 total experts.
skip_conditions: No authority signals matched any available expert

soft_warnings.message: No experts were consulted — the assessment relies entirely on document-extracted baseline entries

phases.name: Merge & Validate
phases.description: Merge baseline and expert entries into unified compliance matrix. Expert entries upgrade baseline entries when source_kind is higher. Generate overlap_keys for cross-authority dedup. Calculate coverage statistics. Validate completeness.

required_fields.message: Compliance matrix must contain at least one merged entry

rules.description: Every 'documented' verdict must have at least one evidence reference
rules.message_template: Entry '{requirement_id}' in '{authority_id}' is marked documented but has no evidence references

rules.description: Every 'partially_documented' verdict must include a gap_description
rules.message_template: Entry '{requirement_id}' in '{authority_id}' is marked partially_documented but has no gap description

rules.description: Every entry with source_kind 'mcp_grounded' or 'document_extracted' must have a source_ref
rules.message_template: Entry '{requirement_id}' in '{authority_id}' has source_kind '{source_kind}' but no source_ref

phases.name: Report Assembly
phases.description: Assemble the final gap analysis report: executive summary, scope and methodology, unified findings register, gap analysis, remediation plan, assumptions and limitations. One report, one deliverable.

required_fields.message: Scope and methodology section is required
Report must be marked as ready

sections: Executive Summary — overall posture, key metrics per authority, critical findings count, confidence assessment
Scope & Methodology — authorities assessed (with versions and sources), documents analyzed, methodology, limitations, client attestations
Unified Findings Register — one table across all authorities with authority, requirement ID, requirement text, verdict, confidence, evidence, gap description
Gap Analysis — critical gaps (contradicted + not_found in laws/regulations), improvement areas (partially_documented), requires human assessment
Remediation Plan — prioritized by authority type (law > regulation > directive > standard > contract > policy > custom) and gap severity
Assumptions & Limitations — every assumption from Phase 1b, every limitation, confidence impact per assumption
deliverable.instructions: Before calling wkfl_export_report, store these keys: org_profile, documents, detected_authorities, scope_readiness, client_questions, assumptions, compliance_matrix, coverage_stats, scope_and_methodology, experts_used, superseded_entries. The tool assembles everything into a single Markdown report. Present the report to the user as the gap analysis deliverable.
